title: Adoption authorities contacts - Canada.ca
content:
## Adoption authorities contacts
Find the various contacts if you want to adopt a child from abroad.

## Government of Canada (federal)
The federal government is only responsible for the immigration or citizenship process of an adopted child.
Under the Hague Convention , Canada’s federal central authority is Intercountry Adoption Services, which is part of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada.
The role of Intercountry Adoption Services is to
- collect and distribute adoption information to Canadian provincial and territorial central authorities foreign authorities
- Canadian provincial and territorial central authorities
- help with communication, cooperation and coordination between federal and provincial and territorial central authorities within the federal government with foreign central authorities or adoption authorities
- between federal and provincial and territorial central authorities
- within the federal government
- with foreign central authorities or adoption authorities
- help to address issues such as unethical and irregular adoption practices
Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Intercountry Adoption Services Family and Social Immigration IA-Inquiries@cic.gc.ca

## Provincial and territorial central adoption authorities
The provinces or territories are responsible for the adoption process.
Choose a province or territory to find its central adoption authority
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Northwest Territories
- Prince Edward Island
Alberta – Alberta Adoption Services, Ministry of Children’s Services
British Columbia – Adoption, Ministry of Children and Family Development
Manitoba – Child Protection Branch, Department of Families
New Brunswick – Child & Youth Services Branch, Department of Social Development
Newfoundland and Labrador – Adoption Services, Department of Children, Seniors and Social Development
Nova Scotia – Adoption Services, Department of Community Services
Northwest Territories – Adoption, Department of Health and Social Services
Nunavut – Department of Family Services
Ontario – Adoption, Ministry of Children, Community and Social Services
Prince Edward Island – Adoption, Department of Social Development and Housing
Quebec – Secrétariat aux services internationaux à l’enfant, Ministère de la Santé et des Services sociaux
Saskatchewan – Adoption, Ministry of Social Services
Yukon – Adoption Services, Government of Yukon Children’s Services
